summary: Machine policy for the notification shown when diagnostic-data consent settings change. Microsoft documents the same device policy path, default, and numeric meanings that the app writes, and the local ADMX confirms the exact value name.

setting.name: Diagnostic Data Change Notifications
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can notify the user when the diagnostic-data opt-in setting changes. This policy decides whether those notifications are shown.
setting.why_people_change_it: Organizations may suppress change notifications to keep the experience quieter or more controlled. Personal users often prefer those notifications to stay visible.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling these notifications reduces interruption and UI noise, but it also removes a transparency signal that tells users their diagnostic-data setting changed.
setting.professional_notes: DataCollection.admx maps this machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableTelemetryOptInChangeNotification with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the notification policy.

allowed_values.label: Notification allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and the change notification can be shown.

allowed_values.label: Notification disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The change notification is blocked by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableTelemetryOptInChangeNotification = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1803 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the policy baseline and not a statement about every possible OEM or enterprise configuration.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want to see when this privacy-related setting changes
avoid_for: Environments that want the quietest possible managed experience
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal behavior for this notification.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the change notification hidden on managed devices.
tradeoffs: Users can still be notified when diagnostic-data opt-in changes.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet managed
avoid_for: Users who want explicit visibility when diagnostic-data choices change

states.rationale: Disable the change notification by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a quieter managed experience and do not want this notification shown.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You value visible alerts for privacy-related configuration changes.
tradeoffs: Users lose a built-in signal that the diagnostic-data opt-in setting changed.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: ConfigureTelemetryOptInChangeNotification
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that controls whether diagnostic-data opt-in change notifications are shown.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableTelemetryOptInChangeNotification with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the DataCollection policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableTelemetryOptInChangeNotification = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Microsoft documents ConfigureTelemetryOptInChangeNotification directly in the System Policy CSP, including the exact DataCollection policy key, default, and 0/1 semantics. The page uses the CSP control name while the local DataCollection.admx confirms the registry value name DisableTelemetryOptInChangeNotification, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: This policy setting controls whether notifications are shown, following a change to diagnostic data opt-in settings, on first logon and when the changes occur in settings. If you set this policy setting to "Disable diagnostic data change notifications", diagnostic data opt-in change notifications won't appear. ... Default Value 0. Allowed values: 0 (Default) Enable telemetry change notifications. 1 Disable telemetry change notifications. Group policy mapping: Name ConfigureTelemetryOptInChangeNotification ... Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection.
validation_proof.notes: The System Policy CSP page explicitly documents the same policy behavior, key path, and 0/1 semantics. The exact registry value name is confirmed by the local DataCollection.admx file as DisableTelemetryOptInChangeNotification.
